title: Part Time Student - Marketing Communications - Waterloo, IA(118582)
description:
As a Part-Time Student - Marketing Communications for JD Engine Works located in Waterloo, IA, you will:

 

• Assist Marketing Communications Specialists and the Trade Show Coordinator with various design and administrative projects
• Edit and design communications for external-facing print and digital projects
• Project management of multiple marketing requests with varying deadlines
• Load product data into web tools: Product Catalog, the Portal, and JohnDeere.com
• Write requisitions and follow up on associated issues with purchase orders
• Perform a variety of administrative tasks for the Marketing organization

 

This position is not available to students on immigration visas. 

What Skills You Need

• Ideally you will be pursuing a degree in marketing, graphic design, digital media, public relations, or journalism/mass communication; others may apply
• Computer experience with Windows operating system
• Experience with Microsoft Office - Outlook, Word, Excel, and PowerPoint (including presentation development)
• Ability to communicate and collaborate with many different team members and functional areas
• Available to work 16-20 hours/week during the academic year
• Available to work 35-40 hours/week during the summer

• Must be a full-time student at a local university or college
• If currently attending a community college, must be planning to pursue a 4-year degree
• Graduation date of May 2028 or later
• Cumulative GPA of 2.8 or above

• Must be able to commute to the work location in Waterloo, IA on a regular basis

 

What Makes You Stand Out

• Strong internal and external communication skills, with the ability to tailor messaging to diverse audiences
• High attention to detail and strong organizational abilities
• Exceptional time management skills, able to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines
• Experience with website maintenance and development
• Skilled in creating and producing digital and printed marketing materials
• Graphic design experience, including coursework or hands-on project work
• Proficiency with the Adobe Creative Cloud, including Photoshop, InDesign, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and Premiere Rush

At John Deere, you are empowered to create a career that will take you to where you want to go while working in an inclusive team environment. Here, you'll enjoy the freedom to explore new projects, the support to think outside the box and the advanced tools and technology that foster innovation and achievement. Additionally, we offer a comprehensive reward package to help you get started on your new career path, including:

  • Flexible work arrangements
  • Paid vacation time
  • Holiday pay
  • Savings & Retirement benefits (401K and Defined Benefit Pension)
  • Healthcare benefits with a generous company contribution in the Health Savings Account
  • Employee Assistance Programs
  • Tuition assistance
  • Fitness subsidies and on-site gyms at specific Deere locations
  • Charitable contribution match
  • Employee Purchase Plan & numerous discount programs for personal use
  • Adoption assistance

 

$15 - $40 hourly based on published rates for business function and education level.

Must be 18 years of age or older to apply
